newsSinceJun6: No company-specific disclosure since June 6. Today's +8.52% to 118,500 KRW is a flow-driven rebound within an extreme retail MLCC theme: the stock hit limit-up on May 22/26/28 (AI-server MLCC shortage narrative, Murata/SEMCO price-hike expectations, Yongin capacity expansion touted as +40-50% output), peaked ~162,800 KRW on May 28, then retraced ~33% into June 8. The bounce coincides with sector commentary that the early-June Korean MLCC sell-off was overdone and the price-hike cycle is strengthening (Samsung Electro-Mechanics-focused notes), not new Samwha fundamentals (no earnings, contracts, or filings found).

sources.label: Mimint - Samwha Capacitor +29.31% close, MLCC/EV flows (May 28, 2026)
Daum/news - Samwha limit-up on MLCC boom hopes, 52-week high (May 22, 2026)
AlphaBiz - SEMCO June pullback overdone, MLCC price-hike cycle strengthening

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved it down for the post-rerating loss of residual alpha; nothing genuinely new since June 6, and today's +8.5% is a no-news flow bounce inside a 33% retracement off the May 28 peak, so the rank-76 Monitor/benchmark slot still fits. Small negative drift because the bounce re-confirms retail crowding without fresh fundamental evidence.
verdicts.bull: AI-server high-capacitance MLCCs are entering genuine shortage, Murata/SEMCO price hikes should trickle down to second-tier Korean makers, and the Yongin expansion could lift output 40-50% with Q1 MLCC revenue already +24% YoY.
verdicts.neutral: The thesis is intact but fully discovered: +293% YTD at a $0.78B cap with repeated limit-ups means the bottleneck story is now consensus retail property; the 33% pullback restored some asymmetry, but today's bounce ate part of that back.
verdicts.bear: A micro-cap riding a theme with three limit-ups in one month is flow, not alpha; if MLCC price-hike pass-through to tier-2 suppliers disappoints in Q2 prints, the stock can retrace another leg with no fundamental floor near these multiples.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 results/2H guidance showing actual MLCC price-hike pass-through and Yongin capacity ramp converting to revenue.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Theme deflation: retail flow reverses and the stock keeps unwinding the May spike faster than earnings can catch up.
